key_insight: DS-HGNN uses physics-guided heterogeneous graph message passing to predict full-field stress and displacement in stiffened panels accurately and efficiently.
engineering_relevance: It enables fast surrogate analysis for complex thin-walled structures during design and optimization, reducing reliance on expensive finite element runs.
method: Panel-level heterogeneous graph representation of the structure
Dual-stream message passing for longitudinal and transverse structural information with cross-stream exchange
FiLM-conditioned 1-D spectral convolutions plus a spectral-bypass low-rank readout for field reconstruction
evidence_signal: Across stiffened panel datasets with varied geometry, loading, boundary kinematics, and nonlinear material response, DS-HGNN achieved the lowest stress and displacement RMSE among six benchmark HGNN models and matched top benchmark accuracy with 19%-38% fewer training samples.
watch_item: Performance may depend on accurate encoding of edge types, boundary kinematics, and training coverage of the geometry/loading space.
paper_title: Physics-Guided Dual-Stream Heterogeneous Graph Neural Network for Predicting Full-Field Structural Response of Stiffened Panels
